summary: Helm chart scaffold fully implemented under charts/memcached-operator/. Includes Chart.yaml (apiVersion v2, type application, version 0.1.0), comprehensive values.yaml with all specified tunables matching config/manager/manager.yaml defaults, _helpers.tpl with standard name/fullname/chart/labels/selectorLabels/serviceAccountName helpers using app.kubernetes.io conventions, CRD in crds/ directory (exact copy of config/crd/bases/), crd-template.yaml gated by crds.managedByHelm, NOTES.txt with post-install instructions, .helmignore with standard exclusions, three helm-unittest test suites (chart metadata, CRD toggle, helper functions), and reference documentation at docs/reference/backend/helm-chart-scaffold.md.

tests_checklist.item: helm-unittest test suites exist for chart metadata, CRD toggle, and helper labels
Tests cover crds.managedByHelm toggle (false=no CRD, true=renders CRD)
Tests cover fullname truncation at 63 characters
Tests verify all standard labels (helm.sh/chart, app.kubernetes.io/*)
Tests use specific assertions (exact values, not just exists)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows standard Helm chart conventions (apiVersion v2, templates/_helpers.tpl)
_helpers.tpl uses idiomatic Helm patterns (trunc 63, trimSuffix, default)
No code duplication in helpers — labels/selectorLabels properly composed
values.yaml has YAML comments documenting every tunable
CRD template correctly gated with {{- if .Values.crds.managedByHelm }}

security_checklist.item: Security contexts match config/manager/manager.yaml (runAsNonRoot, runAsUser 65532, seccompProfile RuntimeDefault)
Container security context restrictive (allowPrivilegeEscalation false, readOnlyRootFilesystem true, drop ALL capabilities)
No hardcoded secrets in chart files
No sensitive data exposed in NOTES.txt

architecture_checklist.item: Chart structure follows Helm best practices (Chart.yaml, values.yaml, templates/, crds/, tests/)
Labels match existing app.kubernetes.io conventions from config/ manifests
Dual CRD strategy (crds/ for install, template for managed upgrades) correctly implemented
Resource defaults match config/manager/manager.yaml exactly (cpu 500m/10m, memory 128Mi/64Mi)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unnecessary template files — only scaffold files as specified
values.yaml contains only tunables specified in the plan

fail_fast_checklist.item: CRD template conditional prevents rendering when disabled
Helm template truncation prevents DNS name violations

defensive_checklist.item: Name helpers truncate to 63 chars (DNS limit)
Fullname handles edge case where release name contains chart name
ServiceAccountName falls back to 'default' when create is false and no name set

suggested_improvements: D2 (minor): image.tag defaults to 'latest' in values.yaml but docs say 'defaults to chart appVersion'. Consider using '""' as default and adding '{{ .Values.image.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}' in the deployment template (when created in a future feature) to match the documented behavior.

D2 (minor): CRD content is duplicated between crds/ directory and templates/crd-template.yaml (~3726 lines each). Consider adding a Makefile target to sync these files automatically when the CRD is regenerated. Out of scope for this feature but worth tracking.

V1 (info): helm lint and helm-unittest could not be run in this environment (helm binary not available). Manual YAML structure verification performed instead. CI pipeline should run these as part of chart validation.
next_steps: Run helm lint and helm unittest in a CI environment with helm installed to get full automated verification
Proceed with N002+ features that build on this scaffold (Deployment template, RBAC, webhooks, Services)
